query_normalized: detecting distribution shift in public health surveillance data via kernel two‑sample tests statistics
rationale: The paper studies detection of distribution shifts (anomalous sequences) using maximum mean discrepancy, a kernel two‑sample test, which matches the methodological mechanism the user seeks (category a). Although it does not focus on public‑health surveillance data, it provides a canonical non‑parametric approach to shift detection that would be cited in a review of kernel‑based two‑sample tests.
